context_of_use: Comparative synthetic engineering evidence for ECG R-peak detector robustness across a paired, escalating calibrated-noise frontier.

acceptance_profile.description: Pooled floor plus pre-specified per-tier gates. Every noisy case is long and separately gated, so clean performance cannot hide a collapsed noise tier.
Clean-anchor performance confirms that noise robustness is not purchased by degrading ordinary detection.
-7 dB target SNR with baseline-wander severity 0.35 and powerline severity 0.10.
-8 dB target SNR with baseline-wander severity 0.50 and powerline severity 0.18.
-9 dB target SNR with baseline-wander severity 0.65 and powerline severity 0.26.
-10 dB target SNR with baseline-wander severity 0.80 and powerline severity 0.34.

truth_policy.event_scoreability: All additive calibrated-noise, baseline-wander and powerline intervals remain scoreable. Only physically truncated record-boundary QRS truth is excluded.
truth_policy.paired_design: The four noisy scenarios use the same cardiac timebase, deterministic PVC cadence, interval schedule and noise source offsets; only target SNR and analytic artifact severities increase.
truth_policy.snr_definition: Target SNR is 20 log10(clean AC RMS / added-noise RMS), calibrated independently for every selected lead and interval. More-negative values are harder.
truth_policy.scope: Only R-peak event files are required. Signal-quality labels and downstream RR/HRV measurements are explicitly outside the evidence matrix.
truth_policy.aggregation: Each SNR case has its own acceptance stratum. Pooled results cannot compensate for a failed tier.

evidence_boundary: Synthetic comparative R-peak detector engineering evidence only; the SNR ladder is not a patient-noise prevalence model, clinical validation, diagnostic performance evidence, or regulatory qualification.
